title: Merge conflict resolver vs conflict resolution vs conflict state vs review before submit
summary: A merge conflict resolver is a source-control-specific resolver for branch, file, and hunk conflicts. Conflict resolution is the broader decision workflow for competing values, conflict state exposes that competing versions exist, and review before submit verifies a user's own captured answers before a transaction.
decisionRules: Use merge conflict resolver when the competing values are source-code branches, files, hunks, conflict markers, and a resolution commit in a repository.
Use conflict resolution when users must decide what survives across records, documents, imports, or synchronized objects without source-control hunk and branch semantics.
Use conflict state when the main UI job is to expose that competing valid versions exist, preserve both values, and route users to the proper resolver.
Use review before submit when users are checking their own captured answers before sending, paying, publishing, or applying a transaction and no external branch or collaborator version competes.

A merge conflict resolver must name target and source branches, file paths, hunk numbers, code context, and commit scope; a general conflict resolver can use field, row, document, or object labels instead.
A merge conflict resolver must validate that conflict markers and unsupported files are resolved before enabling commit; a conflict state may only show the unresolved badge and affected object.
Review before submit can offer Change links for user-entered values, but it should not present Use ours, Use theirs, branch labels, or repository commit messages.
Conflict resolution can include final reviewed summaries, but merge conflict resolver summaries must include files changed, remaining hunks, branch pair, and resolution commit message.
Do not use bulk Accept all incoming for merge conflicts unless the UI shows affected files, branch meaning, discarded code, and a reviewable commit result.
Do not clear merge conflict status until the repository accepts the resolution commit and refreshes mergeability.
failureModes: A general conflict resolver is reused for code and omits branch labels, file paths, hunk numbers, and conflict marker validation.
A conflict state opens a merge page that only says conflicts exist and offers no per-hunk decisions.
A review-before-submit page asks developers to check a merge result but hides source and target branch code.
A merge conflict resolver enables Commit while <<<<<<< markers, unsupported files, or moved-target errors remain.
A bulk incoming choice discards target-branch code across many files without a changed-file summary.
The UI clears conflict status after a browser edit even though the repository rejects the resolution commit.

sources.note: GitLab documents source and target conflict blocks, Use ours and Use theirs choices, inline editing, commit messages, and manual resolution limits.
Microsoft Office conflict review helps distinguish general recovered-change decisions from source-control branch and hunk semantics.
Confluence conflict display helps distinguish document conflict choices from repository merge conflict resolution.
Check answers guidance helps distinguish review-before-submit from conflict resolution between competing versions.
